title: Coelogyne globosa
intro: Coelogyne globosa, also known as the globous coelogyne, is a species of orchid native to tropical regions in Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative damage. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne globosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Coelogyne globosa include teas, infusions, and extracts, often used in traditional medicine to promote relaxation and well-being.
taxonomy: Coelogyne globosa is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family is further divided into various subfamilies, with Coelogyne globosa belonging to the subfamily Epidendroideae. The genus Coelogyne is a group of terrestrial orchids native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Australia. The species Coelogyne globosa is a type of terrestrial orchid that grows in the wild, often in shaded areas. It is a part of the Coelogyne genus, which comprises over 200 species of orchids.
